title: Mid-Market Must Flex More Muscle in IT Outsourcing
author: Rick Saia
subject_domain: IT outsourcing; mid-market enterprises; service level management; governance
methodology: Aberdeen Market Segment report based on survey data from The Business Value of IT Outsourcing Benchmark Report (July 2006)
abstract: Aberdeen Market Segment report analyzing IT outsourcing practices at mid-size enterprises versus Best in Class organizations. Key finding: 25% of mid-market respondents admitted their companies have not been successful with IT outsourcing; none of the Best in Class cited lack of success. Report analyzes four stages prone to planning errors: creation of quantitative business case, contract pricing negotiations, governance, and ongoing operational management. Best in Class outperform mid-market across all four stages. Three recommendations: (1) Get the RFP right — know requirements, costs, and desired advantages; (2) Don't be afraid to look outside for help — use independent consultants and legal assistance; (3) Improve relationship management — dedicate management time and hold one person accountable for the outsourcing relationship.
importance_rationale: Solid primary Aberdeen survey-based research. Moderate importance to the SOA2 archive as this is an IT outsourcing report rather than SOA/technology-architecture focused. Author is Rick Saia (not Kastner). Has value as contextual study of mid-market IT management challenges contemporary with the SOA-era Kastner studies.
relevance_rationale: Adjacent to core SOA archive focus. IT outsourcing is a related IT management domain but not directly SOA/BPM/integration-focused. Relevant as context for understanding mid-market IT challenges in the 2006 timeframe.
prescience_rationale: Observations about mid-market governance weakness in outsourcing management remain relevant. Recommendations around RFP discipline, relationship management, and outside consulting are prescient for current IT services management.
